title: Clip applier comprising a reciprocating clip advancing member
abstract: A surgical device for clipping tissue is disclosed. The surgical device comprises a housing comprising a motor configured to output rotary motions, a shaft, and a clip applier head. The clip applier head comprises a clip magazine, an end effector, a jaw closure cam, and a clip advancing member. The clip magazine comprises a plurality of clips. The end effector comprises a first jaw and a second jaw movable relative to each other between an unclamped position and a clamped position. The jaw closure cam is configured to move the first jaw and the second jaw toward the clamped position. The clip advancing member is operably attachable to the jaw closure cam. The clip advancing member is configured to move a clip from the clip magazine into a firing position. The jaw closure cam and the clip advancing member are configured to move in opposite directions at the same time.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A surgical device for clipping tissue, comprising: a housing comprising a motor configured to output rotary motions; a shaft extending from said housing; and a clip applier head extending from said shaft, comprising: a clip magazine comprising a plurality of clips; an end effector comprising a first jaw and a second jaw, wherein said first jaw and said second jaw are movable relative to each other between an undamped position and a clamped position; a jaw closure cam configured to move said first jaw and said second jaw toward said clamped position; and a clip advancing member operably attachable to said jaw closure cam, wherein said clip advancing member is configured to move a clip from said clip magazine into a firing position, and wherein said jaw closure cam and said clip advancing member are configured to move in opposite directions at the same time. 2. The surgical device of Claim 1 , further comprising a rotary drive, wherein said rotary drive is operably responsive to said rotary motions of said motor, and wherein said jaw closure cam is operably coupled to said rotary drive. 3. The surgical device of Claim 2, wherein said jaw closure cam translates in a distal direction to move said first jaw and said second jaw toward said clamped position in response to said rotary drive rotating in a first direction. 4. The surgical device of Claim 3, wherein said clip advancing member translates in a proximal direction to move a said clip toward said firing position in response to said rotary drive rotating in a second direction. 5. The surgical device of Claim 4, wherein said first direction is opposite said second direction. 6. The surgical device of Claim 2, wherein said clip advancing member comprises a biasing member configured to bias said clip advancing member in a distal direction. 7. The surgical device of Claim 6, further comprising: a latch operably attached to said jaw closure cam, wherein said latch is configured to operably couple said jaw closure cam and said clip advancing member; and 

 a latch release configured to operably decouple said clip advancing member from said jaw closure cam when said clip advancing member is moved into said firing position. 8. The surgical device of Claim 1 , wherein said shaft defines a shaft axis, and wherein said clip magazine is configured to rotate about said shaft axis. 9. A surgical device for clipping tissue, comprising: a housing comprising a motor configured to output rotary motions; a shaft extending from said housing; a clip track comprising a plurality of clips removably stored therein; an end effector extending from said shaft, comprising: a first jaw; a second jaw, wherein said first jaw and said second jaw are movable relative to each other between an undamped position and a clamped position; and a receiving portion at least partially defined between said first jaw and said second jaw; a drive screw operably responsive to said rotary motions; a jaw cam member operably engaged with said drive screw, wherein said jaw cam member is configured to move said first jaw and said second jaw toward said undamped position in response to a rotation of said drive screw in a first direction; and a clip advancing member operably engaged with said drive screw, wherein said clip advancing member is configured to advance a said clip from said clip track into said receiving portion of said end effector in response to said rotation of said drive screw in said first direction. 10. The surgical device of Claim 9, wherein said drive screw comprises a first thread pitch and a second thread pitch that is different than said first thread pitch, wherein said jaw cam member is threadably engaged with said first thread pitch, and wherein said clip advancing member is threadably engaged with said second thread pitch. 11. The surgical device of Claim 9, wherein said drive screw is configured to translate said jaw cam member at a first rate in response to said rotation of said drive screw, wherein said drive screw is further configured to translate said clip advancing member at a second rate in response to said rotation of said drive screw, and wherein said second rate and said first rate are different. 12. The surgical device of Claim 1 1 , wherein said second rate is faster than said first rate. 13. The surgical device of Claim 9, wherein said jaw cam member is configured to translate a first distance in response to said rotation of said drive screw, wherein said clip advancing member is configured to translate a second distance in response to said rotation of said drive screw, and wherein said first distance and said second distance are different. 14. The surgical device of Claim 13, wherein said second distance is longer than said first distance. 15. The surgical device of Claim 9, wherein said jaw cam member moves said first jaw and said second jaw toward said clamped position in response to a rotation of said drive screw in a second direction, wherein said clip advancing member moves toward a proximal position when said drive screw is rotated in said second direction, and wherein said second direction is opposite said first direction. 16. The surgical device of Claim 15, wherein said jaw cam member is configured to translate a first distance in response to said rotation of said drive screw in said second direction, wherein said clip advancing member is configured to translate a second distance in response to said rotation of said drive screw in said second direction, and wherein said first distance and said second distance are different. 17. A surgical device for clipping tissue, comprising: a housing comprising a motor configured to output rotary motions; a shaft extending from said housing; a clip magazine comprising a plurality of clips; a clip applier head extending from said shaft; and a drive screw operably responsive to said rotary motions, wherein said drive screw is configured to: perform a first clip applier head action when said drive screw is rotated in a first direction; and perform a second clip applier head action when said drive screw is rotated in a second direction opposite said first direction. 18. The surgical device of Claim 17, wherein said clip applier head comprises: 

 an end effector comprising a first jaw and a second jaw, wherein said first jaw and said second jaw are movable relative to each other between an undamped position and a clamped position; and a clip advancer movable between an unbiased position and a biased position. 19. The surgical device of Claim 18, wherein said first clip applier head action comprises moving said first jaw and said second jaw toward said clamped position. 20. The surgical device of Claim 19, wherein said second clip applier head action comprises moving said clip advancer toward said biased position.
